titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the James Branch Cabell Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11113660.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday April 22, 2018 at 20:42

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains letters, manuscripts, and printed material related to the life and work of James Branch Cabell. The letters in Series I were written by Cabell to his publishers, literary collaborator, and colleagues; in most cases the folders include carbon copies of responses sent back to Cabell. Writings in Series II are typescripts and proofs of several works, and include correspondence related to their publication, as well as designs by John O'Hara Cosgrave for the cover and end papers of Cabell's work There Were Two Pirates. Series III holds published material about Cabell, including reviews of his work, in the form of clippings, pamphlets, and other ephemera, and portrait photographs by Carl Van Vechten and Foster Studio, Richmond.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was assembled by library staff and was acquired by gift and purchase from various sources. Source information is recorded at the folder level in the contents list. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The James Branch Cabell Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: James Branch Cabell Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collection are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization, in 2012.
This finding aid was produced from previously existing cards in the Manuscripts Catalog. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained.
This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call numbers: Za Cabell, Za C109 +1, Za C109 +S1, Za C109 +G1, and Za Obj Art.

bioghist.head: James Branch Cabell (1879-1958)
bioghist.p: James Branch Cabell, American author of works of fiction and non-fiction, including novels, comedies, short stories, essays, poetry, and writings on genealogy. He was a native of Richmond, Virginia, and a graduate of the College of William and Mary.

For a full biography and bibliography of his work, see Cabell's entry in the online Encyclopedia of Virginia at http://www.encyclopediavirginia.org/Cabell_James_Branch_1879-1958 (accessed November 12, 2012).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains letters, manuscripts, and printed material related to the life and work of James Branch Cabell. The letters in Series I were written by Cabell to his publishers, literary collaborator, and colleagues; in most cases the folders include carbon copies of responses sent back to Cabell. Writings in Series II are typescripts and proofs of several works, and include correspondence related to their publication, as well as designs by John O'Hara Cosgrave for Cabell's work There Were Two Pirates. Series III holds biographical material and published reviews of Cabell's work in the form of clippings, pamphlets, and other ephemera, along with portrait photographs of Cabell by Carl Van Vechten and by the Foster Studio, Richmond.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1918-1958. II. Writings, 1928-1949. III. Printed Material and Photographs, 1912-1971.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent. Additional correspondence related to Cabell's publications is filed in Series II.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of James T. Babb, 1959 and 1961.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of John Chipman Farrar, 1949, 1950, 1955, 1956, and 1958.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 65 letters to Farrar and 1 letter to Margaret Petherbridge Farrar; 3 letters to M. B. Sheffield; 1 letter to J. Kempner; 2 letters from Margaret Freeman Cabell to Farrar; 1 telegram and 3 carbon letters from Farrar to Cabell
Also in the folder is Farrar's correspondence with David L. Barnett, William Lawton Maner, and Josephine Pinkney regarding Cabell.

did.unittitle: Farrar Rinehart, and Rinehart Company, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 112 letters to Farrar & Rinehart (correspondents include: John Chipman Farrar, Stanley M. Rinehart, Philip Wylie, Adelaide A. Sherer, Marguerite J. Reese and William Raney) with carbon copies of the publishers' replies and letters to and from other publishers and agents

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired on exchange from the German Literature Collection, 1967.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased on the George B. Alvord Fund and American Studies' funds, 1965.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 154 letters and 4 telegrams to Hanna, with whom Cabell collaborated on The St. Johns for the "Rivers of America" series; 34 letters and 2 telegrams from Hanna to Cabell; and 1 letter to Mrs. Price

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title. Writings by Cabell that were published in newspapers appear in folders 45 and 46 in Series III.

did.unittitle: The Devil's Own Dear Son , typescript, corrected
The Jewel Merchants , preface, typescript, corrected; signed by Cabell on May 21, 1948
Let Me Lie , typescript, page proofs, and galleys, corrected
Typescripts, corrected and page proofs, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 5 typescript letters to James R. Wells.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Roger W. Straus, John Chipman Farrar, and Julius Fleischmann.

did.unittitle: Postcards, some with notes by Cabell regarding various places and objects in Florida and their use as the setting for the story
Sample book pages and designs for cover, end papers, illustrations, etc., by John O'Hara Cosgrave
did.physloc: Also stored in: Oversize, Box 6, folder 52

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Printed material arranged chronologically, with photographs at the end.

did.unittitle: Clippings and pamphlets by and about Cabell
"Figures of Allegory" by Leon Howard; study of James Branch Cabell reprinted from the Sewanee Review
The Virginia Author 1819-1969 ; University of Virginia Library exhibition checklist
The Cabellian: A Journal of the Second American Renaissance ; vol. 4, no. 1
"The Map of Philip Borsdale, circa 1679," by Frank Cheney Papé

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Title within map image: Poictesme; shows the places mentioned in the novels Figures of Earth , Domeni , Jurgen , The High Place , and The Silver Stallion .

did.unittitle: "James Branch Cabell," by Frank Cheney Papé; portrait etching of James Brand Cabell, signed

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Typscript notation on verso: This is one of the 210 Impressions, of which 200 are for sale, and plate has been destroyed.

did.unittitle: Portrait photographs by Carl Van Vechten (2) and Foster Studio, Richmond
In Series II: There Were Two Pirates , designs for cover, end papers, illustrations, etc., by John O'Hara Cosgrave
In Series III: "The Map of Philip Borsdale, circa 1679," by Frank Cheney Papé
In Series III: "James Branch Cabell," by Frank Cheney Papé; portrait etching of James Brand Cabell, signed
